content:
Character: Oliver Brennan
Perception: Oliver is unassuming — tall, angular, quiet, the kind of guy you'd walk past on the street and never notice, which is a shame because once you get him talking he's one of the most interesting people in this building. He has a sharp, dry wit and a literary mind trapped inside an engineer's career. He's honest in a way that's almost painful — no performance, no posturing. I consider him a real friend. I think he sees me as someone who believes in him, which I do. I know he used to write short stories and that he stopped. I left that August Wilson book in the laundry room on purpose.
summary:
Character: Oliver Brennan
Perception: Oliver is unassuming — tall, angular, quiet, the kind of guy you'd walk past on the street and never notice, which is a shame because once you get him talking he's one of the most interesting people in this building. He has a sharp, dry wit and a literary mind trapped inside an engineer's career. He's honest in a way that's almost painful — no performance, no posturing. I consider him a real friend. I think he sees me as someone who believes in him, which I do. I know he used to write short stories and that he stopped. I left that August Wilson book in the laundry room on purpose.
